Corporate Training Project 01

Breaking the Echo — Overcoming Sycophancy & Confirmation Bias

A fully custom-coded interactive eLearning course challenging sycophancy and confirmation bias in corporate environments. Features gamification, branching scenarios, drag-and-drop activities, a Nokia case study, a 30-day action plan and a personalised certificate.

17+ Slides Truth Seeker Score Drag & Drop Hotspot Activity Scenario Branching Self-Assessment Manager’s Pledge Certificate
Identify cognitive biases and their real organisational cost
Distinguish genuine professional respect from toxic sycophancy
Apply 3 practical tools — Pre-Mortem, Devil’s Advocate, Anonymous Polling
Complete a personal commitment and receive a branded completion certificate
Launch Course
Articulate Storyline 360 United Nations
United Nations Project 02

Development of the Gender & Sustainability Curriculum

An end-to-end eLearning curriculum commissioned for the United Nations, translating complex gender equity and environmental sustainability policy frameworks into structured learning for international staff. Designed to drive inclusive, systemic and lasting institutional change.

UN-Branded Design WEF 2024 Data Zoomable Maps Locked Navigation Reflection Activities
Gender norms restrict access to education, land, water and decision-making
Women form 43% of global agricultural workforce but own less than 20% of land
Gender gap in economic participation only 59% closed globally (WEF 2024)
80% of climate-displaced persons are women and children
View on Articulate Review
Articulate Rise 360 Novonesis
Novonesis Project 03

Understanding Personal Data for Novonesis

A role-based compliance eLearning course on personal data protection and GDPR obligations built for Novonesis employees. Three distinct audience tracks — All Employees, People Managers and Sales & Marketing — each with tailored scenarios driving genuine behavioural change.

3 Role-Based Paths GDPR Compliance Interactive Cards 3 Graded Quizzes Scenario Cases Novonesis Brand
All Employees — recognise when data collection crosses into sensitive territory
People Managers — handle staff health information with correct confidentiality protocols
Sales & Marketing — identify when customer data capture creates personal data risk
All three tracks assessed via graded scenario-based quizzes
View on Articulate Review
